g.text: Digital Credential Issuance (SD-JWT VC, HSM-backed Issuer, Remote Credential Model)
Private key is generated inside HSM
Core stores key metadata and HSM reference
Backend receives ISSUER_KEY_ID and publicKey only
Show consent purpose, terms, version, expiry
Validate issuer eligibility and schema policy
[DATA STORE] Store trust validation result , policy version, timestamp
Validate issuer allowed & schema approved & LoA sufficient & consent valid
GET card / document info from org
[DATA STORE] Store source data freshness and cache policy
sourceSystem = Government API Source of Truth
Validate official data & Check status active & Check not suspended &
retentionPolicy = e.g. 10 years or regulatory requirement
5. Setup Issuer One Time or Reuse
[ERROR AUDIT] Store issuer setup failure audit log
Issuer Key ID (ISSUER_KEY_ID), publicKey or error
[ERROR AUDIT] Store issuer key failure audit log
Do not store HSM_KEY_REF if managed by Core
Do not expose key to mobile app
[SPEC] DID can have multiple keys (verification methods)
Initial key is used at creation
Additional keys can be added via identifier update API
Keys can be rotated without changing DID
[ERROR AUDIT] Store issuer identifier failure audit log
or keep for retry according to key policy
Core manages HSM key reference internally
6. Prepare Holder Wallet and DID Binding
challenge bound to userId + deviceId + issuanceSessionId
from Secure Enclave / Android Keystore / StrongBox
Sign challenge with holder private key
Validate user session & Validate device binding &
Verify signed challenge using public key
Do not store holder private key
Holder public key will be embedded as cnf in issued SD-JWT (holder binding)
Bind holder identifier to user account
Bind holder identifier to trusted device
7. Define Digital Card/Document Schema and Lifecycle Policy
[DATA STORE] Store schema versioning and compatibility policy
migrationPolicy = support old credentials until expiry
deprecationPolicy = announce and sunset by date
revocation status method & credential lifecycle policy
[SPEC] Add new key as verification method
Keep old key for verification of existing credentials
[SPEC] Keep old ISSUER_KEY_ID available for verifying previously issued credentials
Old key status = DEPRECATED, not revoked
Use new ISSUER_KEY_ID for new credential issuance only
Revoke old key only if compromised
cnf = HOLDER public key (from Section 6 holder binding)
Sign SD-JWT (JWS) with issuer signing key
iss = issuer DID , vct = credential type
Core keeps FULL SD-JWT = source of truth (Remote model)
Update status ACCEPTED & Update status ISSUED
[AUDIT LOG] Store signed issuance audit log
Confirm credential is stored in Procivis Core
Core is source of truth for VC payload
Do not persist full VC payload in Backend
VC payload remains in Procivis Core
Backend stores references and metadata only
Mobile stores references and display metadata only
Full VC payload is fetched only during VP preparation after consent
11A.2 Prepare Credential Reference Package for Mobile
11A.3 Send Credential Reference to Mobile Wallet
11A.4 Mobile Wallet Stores Credential Reference Only
Do not store full VC payload
Bind credentialId to holder key reference
Mark credential as available for presentation
[SPEC] Mobile Wallet does not keep VC payload
Mobile keeps only credential reference + metadata
Holder private key remains on device
Credential payload will be retrieved by Backend from Core during VP flow after Holder consent
11A.5 Secure Local Reference Storage and Access Policy
[DATA STORE] Store credential reference in secure local storage
Protect reference access with device security
Prevent export of holder private key
require holder signing before VP submission
11A.7 Backend Records Remote Storage Audit Log
[AUDIT LOG] Store remote credential reference audit log
rendered from local metadata and remote credential reference
